STORM SURVEY CREW FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH A HIGH END EF-0 IN LAKE GENEVA. WINDS UPWARDS OF 80 MPH OCCURRED AND MUCH OF THE DAMAGE WAS TO TREES. LARGE LIMBS AND TREE TRUNKS KNOCKED DOWN OR SNAPPED. TIME IS ESTIMATED OFF OF RADAR.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 2 SSW CAMP LAKE. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND SNAPPED AT WILMOT AND 286TH. REPORT RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AND TREES CONSISTENT WITH A LOW END EF-1 TORNADO OCCURRED IN NORTHERN ILLINOIS JUST SOUTH OF THE WISCONSIN STATE LINE. THIS TORNADO PROCEEDED NE INTO WISCONSIN TOWARDS CAMP LAKE WHERE MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED THAT IS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO AND WINDS OF 80 MPH. THE POINT IS PLACED BETWEEN THE TWO CENTERS OF DAMAGE.

DELAYED REPORT. 9 LARGE TREES FELL ON HOMES. MANY OTHER VERY LARGE TREES FELL WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED IN THE FONTANA AREA.

DELAYED REPORT WITH UPDATES FROM LOCAL FIRE OFFICIALS IN THE FONTANA AREA. DOZENS OF ROADS WERE BLOCKED BY TREES TUESDAY EVENING. THE CLEAN UP EFFORT CONTINUES TODAY, WITH PUBLIC WORKS CONTINUING TO CLEAN UP BLOCKED ROADS AND RESTORE POWER TO PARTS OF THE AREA. AT LEAST 14 HOMES SUSTAINED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE DUE TO LARGE FALLEN TREES. AN ADDITIONAL 10 TO 12 TREES WERE DOWNED IN THE AREA THAT DID NOT FALL ON VEHICLES OR STRUCTURES. TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED OFF LAYING TOWARDS THE NORTHEAST.
